checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent / SalesRevenueServicesNet, FY2012 10-K balance sheet & income statement
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent / SalesRevenueServicesNet, Q3 2015 10-Q comparative FY2014 data

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=17636000 (2010-12-31) growing to Goodwill=65825000 (2012-12-31) and Goodwill=65348000 (2014-12-31), roughly proportionate to SalesRevenueServicesNet=68474000 (FY2010) growing to SalesRevenueServicesNet=212476000 (FY2014), and driven by disclosed acquisitions (8-K/A filings)
evidence.location: Goodwill balance, FY2014 10-K; corroborated by 8-K/A filings dated 2012-03-05, 2012-10-24

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill declined from 44249000 (2012-12-31) to 28753000 (2014-12-31), consistent with ordinary amortization rather than capitalization buildup

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: Income statement & cash flow statement, FY2014 10-K
Income statement & cash flow statement, FY2012 comparative data in FY2014 10-K

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No inventory concept reported (services company); GrossProfit=83909000 / SalesRevenueServicesNet=154512000 = 54.3% (FY2012) vs GrossProfit=115012000 / SalesRevenueServicesNet=212476000 = 54.1% (FY2014)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-267000 (Q1 2014), -403000 (Q2 2014), 935000 (Q3 2014), 2671000 (Q4 2014); annual NetIncomeLoss=2936000 (FY2014) — Q4 alone represents ~91% of full-year net income
evidence.location: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss figures across 2015 10-Q filings referencing prior-year quarters

evidence.quote: OperatingIncomeLoss=564000 (Q1 2012), 542000 (Q2 2012), 936000 (Q3 2012), 2348000 (Q4 2012); annual OperatingIncomeLoss=4390000 (FY2012) — Q4 represents ~53% of full-year operating income

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent held flat at 102000 from 2011-06-30 through 2012-09-30 while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 21007000 (2011-06-30) to 33223000 (2012-09-30)

AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12035000 (2012-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=8871000 (2013-12-31) and AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=8283000 (2014-12-31) even as SalesRevenueServicesNet grew from 154512000 (FY2012) to 212476000 (FY2014)
DeferredRevenueCurrent grew only from 9648000 (2012-12-31) to 10858000 (2014-12-31), a 12.5% increase, while SalesRevenueServicesNet grew 37.5% over the same period (154512000 to 212476000)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-04-26, approximately one month after the original 10-K filing on 2012-03-29
Multiple 8-K/A amendments filed (2012-03-05, 2012-10-24, 2015-08-14), consistent with routine post-acquisition pro forma financial statement supplements rather than restatements

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data available for Assets, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Liabilities, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, GrossProfit, and related concepts spanning 2009-01-01 through 2015-09-30, consistent with the 2016-03-06 cutoff (FY2015 10-K not yet due/filed)
evidence.location: Full financial_series_point_in_time dataset, Q3 2015 10-Q as latest filing

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential under-accrual of expenses (or acceleration of revenue recognition/true-ups) concentrated in the fourth quarter of each fiscal year, producing a disproportionately strong Q4 relative to weak or negative Q1-Q3 results, then reversing/normalizing the following year — consistent with a 'cookie-jar' reserve release or year-end billing true-up pattern common in telecom expense management contracts.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=12035000 (2012-12-31) declining to 8283000 (2014-12-31) despite revenue growth from 154512000 to 212476000

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Insufficient build-up of the bad debt allowance relative to rapidly growing gross receivables in 2011-2012 (allowance flat at $102,000 while AR nearly tripled), potentially understating bad debt expense and overstating net receivables and earnings during that period, before the allowance was later increased sharply in 2014.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=102000 (2011-06-30 through 2012-09-30) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 21007000 to 33223000 over the same span

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables outpacing revenue growth (rising AR/revenue ratio from roughly 0.83x quarterly-annualized in 2012 to over 1.0x by 2015) could reflect slower collections, extended billing terms, or revenue recognized ahead of cash collection/contractual milestones, potentially overstating revenue or receivables quality.

top_signals: Rising accounts-receivable-to-revenue ratio from ~0.83x (2012) to ~1.07x (2015 Q3), suggesting slower collections or aggressive revenue timing
Allowance for doubtful accounts held flat at $102,000 through 2011-2012 while gross receivables nearly tripled, then jumped sharply in 2014 ($1,007,000), indicating possible prior under-reserving

Persistent pattern of disproportionately strong Q4 net income/operating income relative to weak Q1-Q3 results in 2012 and 2014, alongside declining accrued liabilities and deferred revenue as a percentage of growing revenue

2015 swing to significant quarterly losses (Q2: -2,536,000; Q3: -4,502,000) after several years of modest but consistently Q4-loaded profitability, raising the possibility that previously deferred costs/reserves are now being recognized
